FIG \u2014 Banks.lede: M&A activity and record trading revenues dominate; First Hawaiian expands with $2B TriCo deal while JPMorgan reshuffles leadership, and new regulatory guidance on immigrant lending could pressure credit standards.

bullets.text: First Hawaiian Bank acquiring TriCo Bancshares (CA) for $2B, closing by year-end—marks mainland re-entry after BNP Paribas exit.
Morgan Stanley, Goldman Sachs, and JPMorgan all posting record trading revenues; Morgan Stanley's equities trading surged 69% YoY, signaling broad capital-markets tailwind.
Regulators issue guidance requiring banks to identify and monitor lending risks tied to unauthorized workers—compliance signal that could tighten credit for immigrant borrower portfolios.
JPMorgan's Mary Erdoes retiring after board appointed Doug Petno and Troy Rohrbaugh as co-presidents last month.

FIG \u2014 Asset & Wealth Management.lede: Russell Investments sold to CalPERS in major non-profit pivot, while RIA consolidation accelerates and wealth transfer dynamics reshape the advisory landscape.

bullets.text: CalPERS acquires Russell Investments for $580B pension system's multi-asset expansion; Russell sheds debt and goes non-profit, chasing Vanguard's reputational playbook.
RIA M&A heating up: Hightower hits organic growth highs, Choreo closes fourth deal post-SpaceX, ex-Fido CTO raises $7.7M for standalone platform, Wealthfront crosses $100B AUM.
Buffett to distribute Berkshire stake to three family foundations over 8 years, snubbing Gates Foundation; $93T wealth transfer underway but concentration favors already-rich.
LPL Financial recruits $2.4B HNW team from U.S. Bank via Quotient Advisor Partners, strengthening position in high-net-worth segment.

FIG \u2014 Insurance.lede: 5 insurance stories: structural shifts in reinsurance capital sourcing, M&A in underwriting tech, cyber expansion, UK captive market moves, and FDA vaping enforcement exposure.

bullets.text: Cedents shifting to dual-track reinsurers with both traditional and alternative capital access, signaling structural buyer preference change.
Duck Creek acquires Send Technology to build agentic underwriting platform—consolidation play in AI-driven core operations.
Coalition launches $25M cyber product for large enterprises following Allianz partnership; MGAs scaling up-market.
UK government moving toward captive insurance regulation to stem offshore domiciling; regulatory arbitrage play closing.

FIG \u2014 Specialty Finance.lede: Major payments consolidation bid, CMBS credit deterioration, and AI bubble warnings dominate—plus blockchain payments gaining institutional traction.

bullets.text: Stripe and Advent jointly bid $53B+ for PayPal, potentially reshaping fintech payments competitive dynamics and marking a major M&A signal in the sector.
CMBS special servicing rates climbed in June 2026 on distress in retail, office, and lodging—a red flag for securitization credit quality and upcoming servicer workouts.
BIS warns the $1 trillion AI investment boom risks a sharp correction with spillover risks to global finance and the real economy.
Emirates NBD goes live on Partior's tokenized settlement network for real-time USD cross-border payments—institutional blockchain adoption accelerating.

PE & Deals.lede: GP financing innovation drives mega-deals while mid-market contraction signals selective LP capital deployment across PE strategies.

bullets.text: Arctos lands $6.2bn GP financing debut using 'self-liquidating' Keystone strategy that recycles capital across fund cycles without LP capital calls—a structural innovation easing fundraising friction.
Temasek-backed Vertex downsizes Growth Fund III to $250M (down 40%+ YoY), signaling LP pullback from mid-market financial services PE amid tighter valuations.
Carlyle AlpInvest closes $1.7bn second single-asset secondary fund above target, demonstrating sustained LP appetite for concentrated thematic secondaries with transparent returns.
$177trn institutional investor base deepening private markets allocations; GPs lagging on AI adoption, particularly in Europe, creating tech adoption arbitrage.

Markets & Macro.lede: Stripe and Advent eye $53bn PayPal takeover while banks post record earnings but face headwinds; EU deregulation signals competitive reshuffling.

bullets.text: Stripe + Advent International bid $53bn for PayPal; shares surge 19% pre-market as PE appetite for fintech assets intensifies amid valuation pressure.
Major U.S. banks report record Q2 earnings on investment banking and trading strength, but geopolitical risk, rate-cut bets, and CRE exposure could signal peak cycle.
EU moves to relax bank capital requirements, mirroring U.S./UK deregulation and reshaping competitive dynamics for cross-border players.

Newsletters.lede: Wall Street banks post record revenues on trading and M&A strength, while the Fed signals hawkish inflation stance despite June CPI cooling.

bullets.text: Record bank revenues driven by Iran-related trading volatility and elevated M&A activity, with investment banking units seeing outsized gains.
Fed Governor Warsh takes hard line on inflation to Congress; June CPI at 3.5% (above 2% target but down from 4.2% in May) keeps rate-cut timing uncertain.
iShares SGOV ultra-short bond ETF approaching $100B in assets, poised to be first in class to hit milestone as investors seek yield with minimal duration risk.

Career Intel.lede: No stories in this lane today.

Learn.lede: 4 stories spanning incentive misalignment in sports, AI infrastructure financing, legacy tech constraints, and labor market resilience.

bullets.text: Jonathan Ross engineered Groq's $20B NVIDIA deal through creative financing (Grok Bonds) after near-bankruptcy—a case study in startup capital innovation in AI infrastructure.
IBM's earnings miss masks the real problem: its mainframe cash cow is a strategic straitjacket preventing competitive AI investments.
Youth unemployment (20-24) has flatlined despite the AI boom, suggesting labor market resilience or AI's limited displacement effect so far.

all.lede: Regional bank consolidation heats up with First Hawaiian's $2B TriCo deal, while Stripe and Advent's $53B PayPal bid signals major PE appetite for distressed fintech assets.

bullets.text: First Hawaiian Bank acquiring California's TriCo Bancshares for $2B, closing by year-end—marks First Hawaiian's return to mainland U.S. after BNP Paribas exit.
Stripe and Advent Capital jointly bid $53B+ for PayPal; shares surge 19% pre-market, signaling strong PE appetite for large fintech despite no deal confirmed yet.
PayPal buyout chatter reshapes payments competitive landscape as two fintech giants potentially consolidate under private-equity backing.
